basics.name: Suraj Rajendran
basics.label: Computational Biology PhD Student
basics.summary: Ph.D. student in the Tri-Institutional PhD Program in Computational Biology at Weill Cornell Medical College, specializing in computational biology and machine learning.

work.name: Weill Cornell Medical College
work.summary: Engineering foundation models for predictive embryology in IVF procedures and conducting research on data heterogeneity in federated learning.
highlights: Engineered foundation models for predictive embryology in IVF procedures.
Developed structural variant detection methods for short-read cancer data.
Conducted research on data heterogeneity in federated learning and other ML applications.

work.name: Regeneron
work.summary: Reduced text processing time by 95% using NLP and LLMs.
highlights: Implemented analytics and data visualization tools to transform data into actionable insights.

work.name: Wake Forest School of Medicine
highlights: Performed privacy-preserving methods through holographic transformations.
Developed models to predict immunotherapy-related adverse events.
Preprocessed and cleaned large datasets for NLP pipelines.

work.name: Colgate-Palmolive
highlights: Developed NLP pipelines for veterinary note standardization and search.
Constructed an LSTM model for early renal disease prediction.
Tri-Institutional PhD Program in Computational Biology.
Co-mentored by Dr. Fei Wang and Dr. Iman Hajirasouliha.

awards.title: Graduate Research Fellowship (GRFP)
awards.summary: Supporting doctoral research in computational biology.
